part_title: Section Nine: Microbial and Entomopathogenic Control
part_subtitle: Microbial and Entomopathogenic Control of Tuta absoluta

items.text: Chapter Ten
Integrated Management of Tuta absoluta
Introduction

Microbial control relies on the use of microorganisms or their biological products to infect Tuta absoluta, disrupt its physiological functions, or kill it, while reducing dependence on conventional chemical insecticides.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Microbial control relies on the use of microorganisms or their biological products to infect Tuta absoluta, disrupt its physiological functions, or kill it, while reducing dependence on conventional chemical insecticides.

items.text: The principal groups of microbial control agents studied against the pest include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The principal groups of microbial control agents studied against the pest include:

items: 2. Entomopathogenic fungi, such as Beauveria bassiana and Metarhizium anisopliae.
3. Entomopathogenic nematodes, such as species of Steinernema and Heterorhabditis.
4. Insect viruses, particularly granuloviruses belonging to the family Baculoviridae.
5. Symbiotic bacteria associated with nematodes, which remain an area of research not yet fully translated into direct commercial use.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]2. Entomopathogenic fungi, such as Beauveria bassiana and Metarhizium anisopliae.
[[prepared]]3. Entomopathogenic nematodes, such as species of Steinernema and Heterorhabditis.
[[prepared]]4. Insect viruses, particularly granuloviruses belonging to the family Baculoviridae.
[[prepared]]5. Symbiotic bacteria associated with nematodes, which remain an area of research not yet fully translated into direct commercial use.

items.text: These agents differ fundamentally in how they enter the insect:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These agents differ fundamentally in how they enter the insect:

items: The larva must ingest B. thuringiensis bacteria or their toxins.
Fungi penetrate the external cuticle after spores germinate on it.
Nematodes enter larvae through natural openings and then release symbiotic bacteria into the body cavity.
The larva must ingest viral occlusion bodies before infection begins.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The larva must ingest B. thuringiensis bacteria or their toxins.
[[prepared]]Fungi penetrate the external cuticle after spores germinate on it.
[[prepared]]Nematodes enter larvae through natural openings and then release symbiotic bacteria into the body cavity.
[[prepared]]The larva must ingest viral occlusion bodies before infection begins.

items.text: The same rules therefore cannot be applied to all of these organisms, and they cannot be evaluated solely according to speed of kill.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The same rules therefore cannot be applied to all of these organisms, and they cannot be evaluated solely according to speed of kill.

items.text: Importance of Microbial Control in the Management of Tuta absoluta
The importance of microbial agents increases because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The importance of microbial agents increases because of:

items: The pest’s ability to develop resistance to several chemical insecticides.
Larval presence inside mines, reducing exposure to some contact insecticides.
The need for products compatible with egg parasitoids and predators.
The lower residue concern associated with correctly used registered microbial products.
The possibility of targeting several stages of the pest life cycle.
Their role within mode-of-action rotation programs.
Suitability of some products for use near harvest according to the registered label.
Their ability to reduce selection pressure caused by excessive chemical-insecticide use.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The pest’s ability to develop resistance to several chemical insecticides.
[[prepared]]Larval presence inside [[term:mines]]mines[[/term]], reducing exposure to some contact insecticides.
[[prepared]]The need for products compatible with [[term:biological-control]]egg parasitoids[[/term]] and predators.
[[prepared]]The lower residue concern associated with correctly used registered microbial products.
[[prepared]]The possibility of targeting several stages of the pest life cycle.
[[prepared]]Suitability of some products for use near harvest according to the registered label.
[[prepared]]Their ability to reduce selection pressure caused by excessive chemical-insecticide use.

items.text: However, describing a product as “biological” does not automatically mean that it is:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]However, describing a product as “biological” does not automatically mean that it is:

items: Capable of reaching large larvae inside fruits.
Equally effective among all strains and formulations.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Capable of reaching large larvae inside fruits.
[[prepared]]Equally effective among all strains and formulations.

items.text: General Conditions for Success of Microbial Control Agents
Efficacy depends on the interaction of five components.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Efficacy depends on the interaction of five components.

items.text: First: The Pathogen
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items: Viability of spores or infective units.

items.text: Second: Pest Stage
In general:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In general:

items: Young larvae are more susceptible to Bacillus thuringiensis and viruses.
Eggs and exposed larvae may be susceptible to some fungi.
Larvae inside mines may be reached by some nematodes when a suitable water film is present.
Pupae in soil are potential targets for fungi and nematodes.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Young larvae are more susceptible to Bacillus thuringiensis and viruses.
[[prepared]]Eggs and exposed larvae may be susceptible to some fungi.
[[prepared]]Larvae inside mines may be reached by some nematodes when a suitable water film is present.
[[prepared]][[term:pupation]]Pupae[[/term]] in soil are potential targets for fungi and nematodes.

These include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These include:

This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items: Access to growing points and clusters.
Soil treatment when pupae are targeted.

items.text: Fifth: The Surrounding Integrated System
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items: Pesticides used before and after application.

items.text: First: Bacillus thuringiensis
Scientific Definition
Bacillus thuringiensis, abbreviated Bt, is a Gram-positive, spore-forming bacterium that produces protein crystals with toxic activity against specific insect groups during sporulation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bacillus thuringiensis, abbreviated Bt, is a Gram-positive, spore-forming bacterium that produces protein crystals with toxic activity against specific insect groups during sporulation.

items.text: Products intended for lepidopteran larvae use strains or subspecies such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Products intended for lepidopteran larvae use strains or subspecies such as:

items: Other strains or mixtures containing different insecticidal proteins.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Other strains or mixtures containing different insecticidal proteins.

items.text: Commercial products generally contain:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Commercial products generally contain:

items: Spreaders or adjuvants that differ among formulations.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Spreaders or adjuvants that differ among formulations.

items.text: Bt products are not equivalent merely because they carry the same species name. They differ in strain, toxin composition, concentration, formulation, and field persistence.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt products are not equivalent merely because they carry the same species name. They differ in strain, toxin composition, concentration, formulation, and field persistence.

items.text: Mode of Action of Bt
Bt acts primarily as a stomach poison rather than as a conventional contact insecticide.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt acts primarily as a stomach poison rather than as a conventional contact insecticide.

items.text: Its mode of action proceeds as follows:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Its [[term:mode-of-action]]mode of action[[/term]] proceeds as follows:

items: 1. The larva ingests protein crystals and spores while feeding.
2. The crystals dissolve in the alkaline environment of the midgut.
4. Active toxins bind to specific receptors on midgut cells.
5. Pores form or severe membrane damage occurs.
7. Midgut tissue collapses and systemic physiological disruption develops.
8. The larva dies after a period that varies according to dose, instar, and strain.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. The larva ingests protein crystals and spores while feeding.
[[prepared]]2. The crystals dissolve in the alkaline environment of the midgut.
[[prepared]]4. Active toxins bind to specific receptors on midgut cells.
[[prepared]]5. Pores form or severe membrane damage occurs.
[[prepared]]7. Midgut tissue collapses and systemic physiological disruption develops.
[[prepared]]8. The larva dies after a period that varies according to dose, instar, and strain.

items.text: This mechanism explains the importance of the larva ingesting a sufficient dose before it penetrates deeply into plant tissue.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This mechanism explains the importance of the larva ingesting a sufficient dose before it penetrates deeply into plant tissue.

items.text: Cry and Vip Proteins
Bt strains produce different groups of proteins.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt strains produce different groups of proteins.

items.text: Cry Proteins
These generally occur in crystals formed during sporulation and act after ingestion and activation in the midgut.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These generally occur in crystals formed during sporulation and act after ingestion and activation in the midgut.

items.text: Vip Proteins
These are insecticidal proteins produced during vegetative bacterial growth and differ partly in receptors and binding mechanisms from some Cry proteins.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These are insecticidal proteins produced during vegetative bacterial growth and differ partly in receptors and binding mechanisms from some Cry proteins.

items.text: Laboratory studies have shown activity of proteins such as Vip3Aa16 against Tuta absoluta larvae, with clear pathological changes in the midgut.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Laboratory studies have shown activity of proteins such as Vip3Aa16 against Tuta absoluta larvae, with clear pathological changes in the midgut.

items.text: However, results from purified or experimentally produced proteins cannot automatically be converted into field recommendations unless a stable registered formulation exists.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]However, results from purified or experimentally produced proteins cannot automatically be converted into field recommendations unless a stable registered formulation exists.

items.text: Most Suitable Stages for Bt Application
Bt is most suitable for targeting:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt is most suitable for targeting:

items: Second-instar larvae before deep penetration into tissues.
Larvae in superficial mines still feeding close to treated surfaces.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Second-instar larvae before deep penetration into tissues.
[[prepared]]Larvae in superficial mines still feeding close to treated surfaces.

items.text: Practical efficacy generally declines with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Practical efficacy generally declines with:

items: Larval entry into fruits or stems.

items.text: Bt does not provide sufficient direct activity against:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt does not provide sufficient direct activity against:

items: Eggs before hatching, except where newly hatched larvae ingest surrounding residues.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Eggs before hatching, except where newly hatched larvae ingest surrounding residues.

items.text: Importance of Application Timing
Bt timing is determined according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt timing is determined according to:

items: Appearance of the first small mines.
Increased trap catches together with confirmation of eggs on plants.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Increased trap catches together with confirmation of eggs on plants.

items.text: The objective is to establish an effective residue layer on tissues where larvae will begin feeding immediately after hatching.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The objective is to establish an effective residue layer on tissues where larvae will begin feeding immediately after hatching.

items.text: Application should not be delayed until most of the population consists of third- and fourth-instar larvae.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Application should not be delayed until most of the population consists of third- and fourth-instar larvae.

items.text: Required Coverage
Because Bt is non-systemic in conventional applications, efficacy depends on deposition at larval feeding sites.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Because Bt is non-systemic in conventional applications, efficacy depends on deposition at larval feeding sites.

items.text: Coverage should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Coverage should include:

items.text: Uneven coverage leaves untreated plant parts through which larvae can enter without ingesting an effective dose.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Uneven coverage leaves untreated plant parts through which larvae can enter without ingesting an effective dose.

items.text: Spray-Water Volume and Calibration
No single spray-water volume is suitable for all fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No single spray-water volume is suitable for all fields.

items.text: Volume is determined according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Volume is determined according to:

items.text: The selected volume should achieve:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The selected volume should achieve:

items: Deposition on both upper and lower leaf surfaces.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Deposition on both upper and lower leaf surfaces.

items.text: The sprayer should be calibrated using water before the product is added.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The sprayer should be calibrated using water before the product is added.

Bt proteins and spores are degraded by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt proteins and spores are degraded by:

items.text: The protection period is therefore generally shorter than that of many long-residual chemical insecticides.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The protection period is therefore generally shorter than that of many long-residual chemical insecticides.

items.text: Application is often preferable during periods of lower radiation, while considering rainfall or overhead irrigation forecasts and following product instructions regarding reapplication.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Application is often preferable during periods of lower radiation, while considering rainfall or overhead irrigation forecasts and following product instructions regarding reapplication.

items.text: There is no fixed reapplication interval for all products. It may range from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]There is no fixed reapplication interval for all products. It may range from:

items: Other intervals according to the registered label and pest pressure.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Other intervals according to the registered label and pest pressure.

items.text: Spray-Water Quality
Product efficacy may be affected by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Product efficacy may be affected by:

items: Prolonged retention of the mixture in the tank.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prolonged retention of the mixture in the tank.

items.text: The following should be observed:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be observed:

items: Test pH and electrical conductivity when a problem is suspected.
Prepare the mixture shortly before application.
Follow the mixing order stated on the label.
Conduct a small compatibility test when clear data are unavailable.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Test pH and electrical conductivity when a problem is suspected.
[[prepared]]Follow the mixing order stated on the label.
[[prepared]]Conduct a small compatibility test when clear data are unavailable.

items.text: Acids, alkalis, or adjuvants should not be added randomly to a microbial product.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Acids, alkalis, or adjuvants should not be added randomly to a microbial product.

items.text: Evaluation of Bt Efficacy
Immediate larval knockdown and mortality should not always be expected. The first biological effect may be cessation of feeding.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Immediate larval knockdown and mortality should not always be expected. The first biological effect may be cessation of feeding.

items.text: Efficacy is measured through:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Efficacy is measured through:

items: Cessation of expansion of existing mines.
Reduced movement of larvae into fruits.
Reduced fruit infestation in the following generation.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Reduced fruit infestation in the following generation.

items.text: Old mines should not be used as the measure of treatment outcome because they remain visible after larval death.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Old mines should not be used as the measure of treatment outcome because they remain visible after larval death.

items.text: Resistance to Bt
Resistance to Bt proteins can develop when the same strain or toxins are used intensively and repeatedly.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Resistance to Bt proteins can develop when the same strain or toxins are used intensively and repeatedly.

items.text: Resistance management includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Resistance management includes:

items: Avoiding exclusive reliance on Bt throughout the season.
Rotating tools with different modes of action.
Targeting young instars at the registered dose.
Not assuming that changing the trade name changes the toxin group.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Avoiding exclusive reliance on Bt throughout the season.
[[prepared]]Rotating tools with different modes of action.
[[prepared]]Targeting young instars at the registered dose.
[[prepared]]Not assuming that changing the trade name changes the toxin group.

items.text: The product’s strain and toxin profile should be verified, not only its trade name.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The product’s strain and toxin profile should be verified, not only its trade name.

items.text: Second: Entomopathogenic Fungi
Principal Species
The principal fungi studied against Tuta absoluta include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The principal fungi studied against Tuta absoluta include:

items: Other species and strains of Metarhizium.
Cordyceps fumosorosea, formerly known as Isaria fumosorosea.
Other fungi evaluated in laboratory studies with variable results.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Cordyceps fumosorosea, formerly known as Isaria fumosorosea.
[[prepared]]Other fungi evaluated in laboratory studies with variable results.

items.text: Efficacy does not depend on species name alone. Two strains of the same species may differ greatly in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Efficacy does not depend on species name alone. Two strains of the same species may differ greatly in:

items.text: Mode of Fungal Infection
Fungi do not need to be ingested to initiate infection.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fungi do not need to be ingested to initiate infection.

items.text: The process generally proceeds as follows:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The process generally proceeds as follows:

items: 1. A spore adheres to the insect body surface.
2. It absorbs moisture and begins germinating.
3. A germ tube and penetration structure form.
4. Enzymes are secreted to degrade cuticular components.
5. The fungus penetrates the body wall.
7. It consumes nutrients and may produce toxic metabolites.
9. Under suitable humidity, the fungus grows externally on the cadaver.
10. New spores form and may contribute to additional infections.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. A spore adheres to the insect body surface.
[[prepared]]2. It absorbs moisture and begins germinating.
[[prepared]]3. A germ tube and penetration structure form.
[[prepared]]4. Enzymes are secreted to degrade cuticular components.
[[prepared]]5. The fungus penetrates the body wall.
[[prepared]]7. It consumes nutrients and may produce toxic metabolites.
[[prepared]]9. Under suitable humidity, the fungus grows externally on the cadaver.
[[prepared]]10. New spores form and may contribute to additional infections.

items.text: Time to death varies according to strain, concentration, temperature, and insect stage.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Time to death varies according to strain, concentration, temperature, and insect stage.

Adult moths contacting spore-covered plant surfaces.

items.text: Effect of Humidity
Spores require suitable moisture for germination and penetration.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Spores require suitable moisture for germination and penetration.

items.text: This does not mean plants must remain saturated with water. It means that the following are important:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This does not mean plants must remain saturated with water. It means that the following are important:

items: Suitable relative humidity in the boundary layer near the leaf surface.
A sufficient period for spore germination.
Prevention of immediate drying of residues after application.

[[prepared]]Prevention of immediate drying of residues after application.

items.text: Application near evening is therefore more suitable in many environments, provided it is not followed by rainfall or irrigation that washes the product off.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Application near evening is therefore more suitable in many environments, provided it is not followed by rainfall or irrigation that washes the product off.

items.text: Effect of Ultraviolet Radiation
Ultraviolet radiation is one of the principal factors degrading fungal spores on plant surfaces.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Ultraviolet radiation is one of the principal factors degrading fungal spores on plant surfaces.

items.text: The fungus should not be expected to penetrate fruit tissue and reach a deeply concealed larva merely because it was sprayed on the fruit surface.

B. bassiana is among the most extensively studied fungi against the pest.

items.text: Third: Entomopathogenic Nematodes
Definition
Entomopathogenic nematodes are microscopic worms belonging mainly to the families:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Entomopathogenic nematodes are microscopic worms belonging mainly to the families:

items.text: Species studied against Tuta absoluta include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Species studied against Tuta absoluta include:

items: Other species and strains of Steinernema.

items.text: The stage used is known as the:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The stage used is known as the:

items.text: Infective juvenile
This is the stage capable of searching for and entering the host.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This is the stage capable of searching for and entering the host.

items.text: Mode of Infection
Entomopathogenic nematodes function in symbiosis with bacteria:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Entomopathogenic nematodes function in symbiosis with bacteria:

items.text: Infection generally proceeds as follows:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infection generally proceeds as follows:

items: 1. Infective juveniles reach the insect body.
2. They enter through the mouth, spiracles, or anus, while some Heterorhabditis species may penetrate thin regions of the cuticle.
5. The bacteria multiply and cause septicaemia and physiological collapse.
6. The insect usually dies within a relatively short period.
7. The nematodes reproduce inside the cadaver.
8. New generations of infective juveniles emerge when resources are depleted.

[[prepared]]5. The bacteria multiply and cause septicaemia and physiological collapse.
[[prepared]]6. The insect usually dies within a relatively short period.
[[prepared]]7. The nematodes reproduce inside the cadaver.
[[prepared]]8. New generations of infective juveniles emerge when resources are depleted.

items.text: The lethal effect depends on both the nematode and its associated bacteria.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The lethal effect depends on both the nematode and its associated bacteria.

items.text: Foliar Application
Foliar application of nematodes requires:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Foliar application of nematodes requires:

items: Relatively high moisture on the plant surface.
Avoidance of the most intense sunlight and drying periods.
A spray-water volume sufficient to create a temporary water film.
Nozzles and filters that do not damage nematodes.
Continuous gentle agitation of the suspension.
Removal of fine filters that obstruct passage, according to product instructions.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Relatively high moisture on the plant surface.
[[prepared]]Avoidance of the most intense sunlight and drying periods.
[[prepared]]A spray-water volume sufficient to create a temporary water film.
[[prepared]]Nozzles and filters that do not damage nematodes.
[[prepared]]Removal of fine filters that obstruct passage, according to product instructions.

items.text: Application is generally conducted when moisture loss is reduced, such as during the evening, while strictly following the product label.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Application is generally conducted when moisture loss is reduced, such as during the evening, while strictly following the product label.

items.text: Soil Application
The following may be targeted:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following may be targeted:

items: Larvae leaving the plant to pupate.
Stages occurring near the soil surface.

items.text: Efficacy depends on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Efficacy depends on:

items.text: The soil should be moist but not waterlogged, and moisture should persist long enough to permit nematode searching.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The soil should be moist but not waterlogged, and moisture should persist long enough to permit nematode searching.

items.text: Effects of Desiccation and Radiation
Entomopathogenic nematodes are relatively sensitive to desiccation and ultraviolet radiation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Entomopathogenic nematodes are relatively sensitive to desiccation and ultraviolet radiation.

items.text: Rapid drying causes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Rapid drying causes:

items.text: Foliar applications in open fields are therefore more challenging than applications in greenhouses or moist soil.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Foliar applications in open fields are therefore more challenging than applications in greenhouses or moist soil.

items.text: Moisture-retaining adjuvants may be used, but they should be:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Moisture-retaining adjuvants may be used, but they should be:

items.text: Variation Among Strains
Tests have shown that local strains may differ from commercial or foreign strains in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tests have shown that local strains may differ from commercial or foreign strains in:

items.text: In a laboratory study in Rwanda, local strains killed Tuta absoluta larvae inside mines, and some local strains outperformed foreign strains during the initial evaluation stages.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In a laboratory study in Rwanda, local strains killed Tuta absoluta larvae inside mines, and some local strains outperformed foreign strains during the initial evaluation stages.

items.text: The researchers nevertheless emphasized the need for field trials before results are generalized.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The researchers nevertheless emphasized the need for field trials before results are generalized.

items.text: Egyptian Trial of Combined Biological Control
An Egyptian study in commercial greenhouses tested the combination of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An Egyptian study in commercial greenhouses tested the combination of:

items.text: The two agents were used alternately during the season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The two agents were used alternately during the season.

items.text: The program produced a gradual reduction in Tuta absoluta abundance compared with the untreated greenhouse.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The program produced a gradual reduction in Tuta absoluta abundance compared with the untreated greenhouse.

items.text: The study demonstrates the possibility of combining an egg-targeting agent with a larval-targeting agent, but it was conducted:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The study demonstrates the possibility of combining an egg-targeting agent with a larval-targeting agent, but it was conducted:

items.text: It therefore provides preliminary operational evidence rather than a fixed dosage program for all Egyptian conditions.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It therefore provides preliminary operational evidence rather than a fixed dosage program for all Egyptian conditions.

items.text: Nematode Quality
The product should be inspected before use through:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The product should be inspected before use through:

items: Absence of abnormal odor or contamination.
Confirmation that the package was not exposed to freezing or excessive heat.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Confirmation that the package was not exposed to freezing or excessive heat.

items.text: A small sample may be examined under suitable magnification to confirm that most individuals are:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A small sample may be examined under suitable magnification to confirm that most individuals are:

items.text: Nematode Tank Mixing
Nematodes should not be mixed with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nematodes should not be mixed with:

items: Pesticides or fertilizers of unknown compatibility.
Harmful concentrations of acids or alkalis.
Materials that clog or kill them.

items.text: Product-specific compatibility charts should be consulted because some pesticides may be compatible at specified concentrations while others are highly harmful.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Product-specific compatibility charts should be consulted because some pesticides may be compatible at specified concentrations while others are highly harmful.

items.text: Fourth: Insect Viruses
Baculoviruses
The principal viruses studied against Tuta absoluta belong to the family:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The principal viruses studied against Tuta absoluta belong to the family:

items.text: Baculoviridae
Particularly the genus:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Particularly the genus:

items.text: Betabaculovirus
Isolates studied include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Isolates studied include:

items: Granuloviruses isolated from Tuta absoluta, referred to in some studies as TuabGV.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Granuloviruses isolated from Tuta absoluta, referred to in some studies as TuabGV.

items.text: These viruses are relatively highly specific to particular groups of Lepidoptera.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These viruses are relatively highly specific to particular groups of [[term:classification]]Lepidoptera[[/term]].

items.text: Mode of Viral Infection
The larva must ingest viral occlusion bodies.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The larva must ingest viral occlusion bodies.

items.text: The infection process proceeds as follows:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The infection process proceeds as follows:

items: 1. The larva consumes occlusion bodies with leaf tissue.
2. The protein matrix dissolves in the alkaline gut.
7. Larval growth and feeding gradually stop.
8. The larva dies after a period depending on dose, instar, and isolate.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. The larva consumes occlusion bodies with leaf tissue.
[[prepared]]2. The protein matrix dissolves in the alkaline gut.
[[prepared]]7. Larval growth and feeding gradually stop.
[[prepared]]8. The larva dies after a period depending on dose, instar, and isolate.

items.text: The virus generally acts more slowly than chemical neurotoxic insecticides, but feeding may stop before complete death.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The virus generally acts more slowly than chemical neurotoxic insecticides, but feeding may stop before complete death.

items.text: Susceptibility of Larval Instars
Young larvae are generally more susceptible to granuloviruses because:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Young larvae are generally more susceptible to granuloviruses because:

items: They require a relatively smaller dose.
They ingest residues when feeding begins.
They have not yet entered deep mines or fruits.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]They have not yet entered deep mines or fruits.

items.text: Advanced larvae:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Advanced larvae:

items: May continue causing damage before death.
Are more difficult to reach with spray deposits.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Are more difficult to reach with spray deposits.

items.text: Successful use therefore depends on application before or at the beginning of hatching.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Successful use therefore depends on application before or at the beginning of hatching.

items.text: PhopGV Isolates
Studies have demonstrated substantial variation among PhopGV isolates in their capacity to infect Tuta absoluta.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Studies have demonstrated substantial variation among PhopGV isolates in their capacity to infect Tuta absoluta.

items.text: Some African isolates were more effective than others against young larvae.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Some African isolates were more effective than others against young larvae.

items.text: This confirms that:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This confirms that:

items: Virus name alone does not determine efficacy.
The product should contain an isolate tested against Tuta.
A dose from a product intended for another pest should not automatically be transferred to Tuta.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Virus name alone does not determine efficacy.
[[prepared]]The product should contain an isolate tested against Tuta.
[[prepared]]A dose from a product intended for another pest should not automatically be transferred to Tuta.

items.text: Persistence of Viruses on Plants
Occlusion bodies are affected by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Occlusion bodies are affected by:

items.text: Programs may therefore require repeated applications during hatching waves according to the product label and monitoring data.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Programs may therefore require repeated applications during hatching waves according to the product label and monitoring data.

items.text: UV protectants or improved formulations may increase persistence, but they should be part of a tested and registered product.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]UV protectants or improved formulations may increase persistence, but they should be part of a tested and registered product.

items.text: Compatibility with Parasitoids
Granuloviruses are highly specific and can theoretically be integrated with egg and larval parasitoids.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Granuloviruses are highly specific and can theoretically be integrated with egg and larval parasitoids.

items.text: A study examining interactions between PhopGV and the larval parasitoids:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A study examining interactions between PhopGV and the larval parasitoids:

items.text: Found that indirect effects were generally limited, although some interactions varied according to parasitoid species and timing of infection.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Found that indirect effects were generally limited, although some interactions varied according to parasitoid species and timing of infection.

items.text: This indicates potential compatibility, but application sequence matters because early larval death may affect a parasitoid developing within or on the host.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This indicates potential compatibility, but application sequence matters because early larval death may affect a parasitoid developing within or on the host.

items.text: Limitations of Viral Control
Principal limitations include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Principal limitations include:

items: Limited availability of registered products in some countries.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Limited availability of registered products in some countries.

items.text: Viruses are therefore integrated with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Viruses are therefore integrated with:

items.text: Fifth: Symbiotic Bacteria Associated with Nematodes
Xenorhabdus and Photorhabdus
Steinernema species are associated with Xenorhabdus bacteria, whereas Heterorhabditis species are associated with Photorhabdus bacteria.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Steinernema species are associated with Xenorhabdus bacteria, whereas Heterorhabditis species are associated with Photorhabdus bacteria.

items.text: These bacteria produce:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These bacteria produce:

items: Metabolites that help kill the host and preserve the cadaver for nematode development.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Metabolites that help kill the host and preserve the cadaver for nematode development.

items.text: Bacterial cells or cell-free filtrates have been tested against Tuta absoluta in laboratory studies.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bacterial cells or cell-free filtrates have been tested against Tuta absoluta in laboratory studies.

items.text: However, the use of bacterial extracts or bacteria separately from nematodes remains a research area and is not a ready field alternative unless a registered and tested product exists.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]However, the use of bacterial extracts or bacteria separately from nematodes remains a research area and is not a ready field alternative unless a registered and tested product exists.

items.text: Bacterial extracts should not be prepared or used locally without evaluation of toxicity, stability, and effects on non-target organisms.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bacterial extracts should not be prepared or used locally without evaluation of toxicity, stability, and effects on non-target organisms.

items.text: Sixth: Compatibility and Integration
Compatibility Between Bt and Natural Enemies
Bt is relatively more selective than many broad-spectrum insecticides because it mainly targets larvae that ingest the active proteins.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt is relatively more selective than many broad-spectrum insecticides because it mainly targets larvae that ingest the active proteins.

items.text: However, compatibility should not be assumed automatically. The following may affect natural enemies:

Fungi can be integrated with parasitoids, but fungal spores may infect:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fungi can be integrated with parasitoids, but fungal spores may infect:

items: Immature parasitoids inside the host indirectly.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Compatibility depends on:

items.text: A time interval between application and release may be necessary.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A time interval between application and release may be necessary.

items.text: Compatibility with Fungicides
Fungicides are among the principal constraints on entomopathogenic fungi.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fungicides are among the principal constraints on entomopathogenic fungi.

items.text: They may:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They may:

items.text: Entomopathogenic fungi should not be tank-mixed with a fungicide unless the product label or compatibility tests confirm safety.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Entomopathogenic fungi should not be tank-mixed with a fungicide unless the product label or compatibility tests confirm safety.

items.text: Existing fungicide residues on leaves should also be considered, not only direct tank mixing.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Existing fungicide residues on leaves should also be considered, not only direct tank mixing.

Possible outcomes include:

Antagonism when one formulation contains materials harmful to the other organism.

[[prepared]]Antagonism when one formulation contains materials harmful to the other organism.

items.text: They should not be mixed automatically in the same tank. The following should be determined:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They should not be mixed automatically in the same tank. The following should be determined:

items.text: Separate application may be safer than direct mixing.

items.text: Compatibility with Copper and Sulfur
Some copper and sulfur products may affect the viability of entomopathogenic fungi or nematodes depending on concentration, formulation, and timing.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Some copper and sulfur products may affect the viability of entomopathogenic fungi or nematodes depending on concentration, formulation, and timing.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should therefore be observed:

Test viability after exposure in research programs.

items.text: Seventh: Operational Management
Selection of a Microbial Agent
Selection is guided by pest status:

rows: Bt or virus covering the hatching site, together with egg parasitoids
Bt, virus, or a registered fungus
Tested foliar nematodes or a suitable fungus
A fungus or nematode registered for soil application
A registered product with a suitable preharvest interval
An agent demonstrated to be compatible with the parasitoid

Storage requirements differ greatly among products.

4. Add the product according to label instructions.
8. Use the mixture within the specified period.
9. Do not leave it until the following day.

items.text: Daily Application Timing
Application near evening is suitable in many cases because it may:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Application near evening is suitable in many cases because it may:

items: Reduce direct exposure to ultraviolet radiation.
Extend the period of surface moisture.
Improve survival of fungi and nematodes.
Coincide with activity of some pest stages.

items.text: However, the following should be considered:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]However, the following should be considered:

items: Avoidance of humidity levels that sharply increase disease risk.

items.text: No single time rule applies to all agents because Bt, fungi, and nematodes have different requirements.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No single time rule applies to all agents because Bt, fungi, and nematodes have different requirements.

items.text: Number of Applications
The number of applications should not be fixed in advance without monitoring.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The number of applications should not be fixed in advance without monitoring.

items.text: It is affected by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It is affected by:

items.text: Reapplication is justified when data show:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Reapplication is justified when data show:

items: Expiry of the product’s protection period.

items.text: It should not be repeated automatically when no active infestation is present.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It should not be repeated automatically when no [[term:infestation-status]]active infestation[[/term]] is present.

Evaluation timing depends on the speed of action of the agent.

Focus on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Focus on:

Allow sufficient time for infection and death and confirm fungal growth where necessary.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Allow sufficient time for infection and death and confirm fungal growth where necessary.

Assess nematode survival and larval infection during the following days.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Assess nematode survival and larval infection during the following days.

items.text: Viruses
Expect a slower effect and assess larvae and the following generation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Expect a slower effect and assess larvae and the following generation.

items.text: These agents should not all be compared after only 24 hours.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These agents should not all be compared after only 24 hours.

These include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These include:

items.text: Calculation of Efficacy
Abbott’s formula may be used in experiments that include an untreated control:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Abbott’s formula may be used in experiments that include an untreated control:

items.text: Corrected efficacy (%) = [(Survival in the control − Survival in the treatment) ÷ Survival in the control] × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Corrected efficacy (%) = [(Survival in the control − Survival in the treatment) ÷ Survival in the control] × 100

items.text: In commercial fields, it is preferable to use indicators including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In commercial fields, it is preferable to use indicators including:

items.text: Laboratory mortality alone is insufficient to predict field return.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Laboratory mortality alone is insufficient to predict field return.

items.text: Economic Cost
The cost of microbial control includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The cost of microbial control includes:

items.text: Benefits include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Benefits include:

items.text: Calculate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Calculate:

items.text: Benefit-to-cost ratio = Value of benefits achieved ÷ Program cost
A treatment is economically viable when the value of benefits clearly exceeds the cost.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A treatment is economically viable when the value of benefits clearly exceeds the cost.

items.text: Common Errors
Error One: Treating All Bt Products as Equivalent
Strains, proteins, and formulations differ.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Strains, proteins, and formulations differ.

items.text: Error Two: Applying Bt After Most Larvae Have Entered Fruits
Bt must be ingested and is more effective against young instars.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt must be ingested and is more effective against young instars.

items.text: Error Three: Evaluating Bt After Only a Few Hours
Feeding may stop before mortality is complete.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Feeding may stop before mortality is complete.

items.text: Error Four: Using a Dose Below the Registered Rate
This may cause treatment failure and increase resistance risk.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may cause treatment failure and increase resistance risk.

items.text: Error Five: Applying Fungi During the Most Intense Sunlight and Dryness
This reduces spore viability and persistence.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This reduces spore viability and persistence.

items.text: Error Six: Mixing a Fungus with a Fungicide
This may prevent germination or kill spores.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may prevent germination or kill spores.

items.text: Error Seven: Generalizing the Result of One Strain to the Entire Species
Strains differ substantially in virulence.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Strains differ substantially in virulence.

items.text: Error Eight: Assuming Endophytic Colonization Is Guaranteed
It depends on strain, application method, plant, and time.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It depends on strain, application method, plant, and time.

items.text: Error Nine: Applying Nematodes to a Dry Surface
Infective juveniles require a moisture film for movement and survival.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infective juveniles require a moisture film for movement and survival.

items.text: Error Ten: Using Nozzles or Filters That Damage Nematodes
Application-equipment specifications should be reviewed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Application-equipment specifications should be reviewed.

items.text: Error Eleven: Exposing Nematodes or Spores to Heat During Transport
This reduces viability before application.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This reduces viability before application.

items.text: Error Twelve: Applying Viruses Only Against Large Larvae
Young instars are more susceptible and easier to reach.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Young instars are more susceptible and easier to reach.

items.text: Error Thirteen: Measuring Success Only by Numbers of Dead Insects
The final indicator is reduced fruit infestation and rejected yield.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The final indicator is reduced fruit infestation and rejected yield.

items.text: Error Fourteen: Leaving a Biological Mixture in the Tank Until the Following Day
The agent’s viability may decline.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The agent’s viability may decline.

items.text: Error Fifteen: Using an Unregistered Product
Research results alone do not demonstrate the safety and efficacy of a commercial product.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Research results alone do not demonstrate the safety and efficacy of a commercial product.

items.text: Practical Priorities for Open-Field Tomatoes in Egypt
Scientific priorities are:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Scientific priorities are:

items: 1. Use only products registered for the crop and pest.
2. Identify the strain and concentration, not only the organism name.
3. Target eggs close to hatching and young larvae.
4. Link application to trap catches and field inspection.
5. Improve coverage of growing points and lower leaf surfaces.
6. Apply fungi and nematodes when desiccation and radiation are reduced.
8. Do not mix fungi with fungicides without compatibility data.
9. Prevent exposure of living products to excessive heat during transport and storage.
10. Evaluate Egyptian or locally adapted fungal and nematode strains.
11. Study soil application of fungi or nematodes against pupae within integrated programs.
12. Build on the Egyptian trial combining T. euproctidis with H. bacteriophora through broader repeated trials.
13. Do not transfer greenhouse results directly to open fields.
14. Evaluate fruit infestation and marketable yield.
15. Document cost, return, and the number of chemical sprays avoided.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Use only products registered for the crop and pest.
[[prepared]]2. Identify the strain and concentration, not only the organism name.
[[prepared]]3. Target eggs close to hatching and young larvae.
[[prepared]]4. Link application to trap catches and field inspection.
[[prepared]]5. Improve coverage of growing points and lower leaf surfaces.
[[prepared]]6. Apply fungi and nematodes when desiccation and radiation are reduced.
[[prepared]]8. Do not mix fungi with fungicides without compatibility data.
[[prepared]]9. Prevent exposure of living products to excessive heat during transport and storage.
[[prepared]]10. Evaluate Egyptian or locally adapted fungal and nematode strains.
[[prepared]]11. Study soil application of fungi or nematodes against pupae within integrated programs.
[[prepared]]12. Build on the Egyptian trial combining T. euproctidis with H. bacteriophora through broader repeated trials.
[[prepared]]13. Do not transfer greenhouse results directly to open fields.
[[prepared]]14. Evaluate fruit infestation and marketable yield.
[[prepared]]15. Document cost, return, and the number of chemical sprays avoided.

items.text: Comparative Table of Microbial Agents
Seasonal Microbial-Control Program
Before Planting
At First Moth and Egg Detection

items: Prepare the registered Bt or virus product.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prepare the registered Bt or virus product.

items.text: At Appearance of L1 and L2

items: Apply Bt or the appropriate microbial agent.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Apply Bt or the appropriate microbial agent.

items.text: When Conditions Are Suitable for Fungi

items: Apply when radiation and desiccation are reduced.
Do not mix with an incompatible fungicide.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Apply when radiation and desiccation are reduced.
[[prepared]]Do not mix with an incompatible fungicide.

items.text: When Larvae Occur Inside Mines

items: Evaluate the result under field conditions.

items.text: When Soil Pupation Is High

items: Evaluate soil treatment with a registered fungus or nematode.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Evaluate soil treatment with a registered fungus or nematode.

items.text: Near Harvest

items: Do not apply a slow-acting treatment that cannot protect a crop approaching imminent harvest.
Focus on preventing infestation of the remaining fruits.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Do not apply a slow-acting treatment that cannot protect a crop approaching imminent harvest.
[[prepared]]Focus on preventing infestation of the remaining fruits.

items.text: Summary of Section Nine
Microbial control of Tuta absoluta includes entomopathogenic bacteria, fungi, nematodes, and viruses.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Microbial control of Tuta absoluta includes entomopathogenic bacteria, fungi, nematodes, and viruses.

items.text: Bacillus thuringiensis is the principal bacterial agent used. It acts after larvae ingest toxic proteins and therefore provides the best results when young larvae are targeted before they penetrate deeply into leaves and fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bacillus thuringiensis is the principal bacterial agent used. It acts after larvae ingest toxic proteins and therefore provides the best results when young larvae are targeted before they penetrate deeply into leaves and fruits.

items.text: Bt products are not equivalent. Subspecies, strains, proteins, and formulations differ, and efficacy is affected by coverage, radiation, rainfall, and larval age.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bt products are not equivalent. Subspecies, strains, proteins, and formulations differ, and efficacy is affected by coverage, radiation, rainfall, and larval age.

items.text: Entomopathogenic fungi infect insects by penetrating the cuticle. Important species include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Entomopathogenic fungi infect insects by penetrating the cuticle. Important species include:

items.text: Fungi require viable spores and suitable humidity and temperature and are affected by ultraviolet radiation and fungicides.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fungi require viable spores and suitable humidity and temperature and are affected by ultraviolet radiation and fungicides.

items.text: Experimental evidence has demonstrated the possibility of targeting Tuta pupae in soil using B. bassiana and M. anisopliae. In addition, M. anisopliae strain ICIPE 20 produced positive economic results in a two-season field trial.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Experimental evidence has demonstrated the possibility of targeting Tuta pupae in soil using B. bassiana and M. anisopliae. In addition, M. anisopliae strain ICIPE 20 produced positive economic results in a two-season field trial.

items.text: Some entomopathogenic nematode strains can reach larvae inside mines when moisture is available and act through symbiotic bacteria belonging to the genera Xenorhabdus and Photorhabdus.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Some entomopathogenic nematode strains can reach larvae inside mines when moisture is available and act through symbiotic bacteria belonging to the genera Xenorhabdus and Photorhabdus.

items.text: Foliar nematode applications require protection from desiccation and radiation, and laboratory results should not be generalized without field verification.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Foliar nematode applications require protection from desiccation and radiation, and laboratory results should not be generalized without field verification.

items.text: Granuloviruses are highly specific agents and perform best when ingested by young larvae, but they act more slowly and may require suitable coverage, timing, and repeated application in some programs.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Granuloviruses are highly specific agents and perform best when ingested by young larvae, but they act more slowly and may require suitable coverage, timing, and repeated application in some programs.

items.text: The best microbial-control results are achieved when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The best microbial-control results are achieved when:

items: The susceptible pest stage is targeted.
A registered strain and product are used.
Products are integrated with parasitoids, predators, pheromones, and agricultural sanitation.
Performance is evaluated using reductions in fruit infestation and economic return.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A registered strain and product are used.
[[prepared]]Products are integrated with parasitoids, predators, [[term:pheromone-trap]]pheromones[[/term]], and agricultural sanitation.
[[prepared]]Performance is evaluated using reductions in fruit infestation and economic return.
